FAQ

Questions we get the most.

Is LandZero an investment platform? +

No. LandZero is a land sales company. We help you own physical, verified land through outright purchase or a flexible payment plan. We do not manage funds or promise returns.

What title documents do I get? +

Depending on the estate, you'll receive a Certificate of Occupancy (C of O), Governor's Consent, or a Gazette, along with your Deed of Assignment and survey plan.

Can I inspect before I commit? +

Yes, always. We encourage every buyer to inspect a plot in person, or join a scheduled group inspection, before making any payment.

How does ZeroPay work? +

ZeroPay lets you spread the cost of a plot into daily, weekly, or monthly instalments that fit your income, with your balance and progress tracked throughout the plan.

What is Landlord Ajo? +

Landlord Ajo is a community ownership model inspired by traditional Ajo. A group of contributors takes turns receiving a fully allocated plot until everyone in the circle owns land.

Why ZeroPay

Built around how you actually get paid.

No hidden markup

What you see is what you pay, spread across your plan. No surprise charges at the end.

Track it yourself

See exactly how much you've paid and what's left, whenever you want.

Miss a day, not your plot

Life happens. A missed payment doesn't cancel your plan, it just extends your timeline.

Powered by LandZero

Own land together with a circle you trust, the traditional Ajo way.

A group of contributors, one plot allocated at a time, until everyone in the circle owns.

How It Works

A trusted circle. One plot at a time, until everyone owns.

Form or join a circle

A group of contributors, often a cooperative, church group, or trusted friends, comes together with a shared savings goal.

Contribute on a fixed schedule

Every member pays the same amount on the same schedule into the circle's pool, fully tracked and visible to all members.

One member is allocated a plot

Each cycle, the pooled contributions fund a verified plot for one member of the circle, on a transparent, pre-agreed rotation.

The circle continues until everyone owns

The cycle repeats until every member in the circle has been allocated their own plot of land.

Built for groups

Cooperative societies, religious organisations, and community groups are the natural fit. Everyone contributes, everyone eventually owns.

No interest, no markup

This isn't a loan. It's structured collective saving. What the circle pays in is what pays for each member's plot.

Start a Landlord Ajo Circle
